channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Rocket League Paris Major Esports Bundle Announced
item.content:encoded:
Rocket League has revealed a new bundle of cosmetics which will directly support esports teams competing at the Paris Major.

A full 100% share of the bundle’s net sales, proceeds after taxes, will be split between the best-performing individual players and the best-performing teams.

The more in-game bundles that are sold, the higher the prize pool will be for the RLCS event, which begins May 20.

This is a common strategy used by esports game publishers; just recently, Riot Games announced 2XKO at Evo will have a $75k prize pool thanks to the Frame Rate esports skin line.

The Rocket League Paris Major Bundle will cost 1,000 in-game credits (around $9.99 USD) and will feature:

  • Shot Caller Decal
  • Heat Lightning Wheels
  • Slay Maker Boost
  • “Surge” Player anthem – Habstrakt & Lizdek

The bundle will be purchasable in Rocket League from May 19 to May 30.

And it has also been confirmed that the publishers will release a similar bundle to support the RLCS World Championship 2026 later this year.

Epic Games and Psyonix have revealed a full breakdown of how the bundle’s net sales will be split between individual and team rewards.

The first 50% of all net sales will go to individual players through Player Performance Awards, spread equally across three categories:

  • •Striker (Highest average goals per game)
  • •Playmaker (Highest average assist per game)
  • •Savior (Most saves per goal allowed)

Then, the other 50% will be split between the 16 teams at the Paris Major, with the higher-placing teams earning a larger share.

That 50% of revenue will be split as follows:

  • 1st – 9.33%
  • 2nd – 9.33%
  • 3rd – 9.33%
  • 4th – 6.75%
  • 5th – 6.75%
  • 6th – 6.75%
  • 7th – 6.75%
  • 8th – 6.00%
  • 9th – 6.00%
  • 10th – 6.00%
  • 11th – 6.00%
  • 12th – 4.20%
  • 13th – 4.20%
  • 14th – 4.20%
  • 15th – 4.20%
  • 16th – 4.20%

What team are you rooting for at the Rocket League Paris Major? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Epic Games is making big moves to please the community, having also added anti-cheat in Rocket League recently.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/rocket-league-paris-major-esports-bundle-announced/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fallout Season 3 Secures Breaking Bad Star
item.content:encoded:
Amazon Prime Video’s Fallout series will be adding Breaking Bad’s Aaron Paul to the cast with Season 3.

It has been a while since Season 2 concluded, and players are eagerly anticipating the next season to continue the story. Ahead of its release, one of the stars has addressed whether the live-action series can be considered Fallout 5. Additionally, we have been informed about a significant new addition to the star cast.

Fallout Season 3 Will Feature Aaron Paul

A new report by Deadline reveals that Aaron Paul will be joining the cast for Fallout Season 3. Paul’s role in the upcoming season wasn’t revealed in the report. However, fans were informed that Annabel O’Hagan (Steph Harper) and Dave Register (Chet) have been made series regulars. Frances Turner (Barb Howard) will also remain as one of the regulars on the show. Moreover, Aaron Paul has already worked with the producers at Kilter Films on Westworld.

Aaron Paul is a well-known figure in the TV industry, largely thanks to his role as Jesse Pinkman in Breaking Bad. However, the Emmy-winning actor has plenty more movies and TV shows under his belt, including BoJack Horseman, Black Mirror, Need for Speed, and more. Fans of the actor will definitely be excited to see what character he brings to life in the new season and how that plays into the story.

In other news, there’s a new mod for Fallout 4 that allows users to play Fallout 1 via their in-game Pip-Boy. Additionally, a Fallout Nuka-Girl Statue is on the way as the fifth installment from Gaming Heads. What are your thoughts on Aaron Paul joining Fallout Season 3? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Nintendo’s stock plunging following a disappointing forecast. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Team Heretics Reunite Nukeduck with Mithy as LEC Head Coach
item.content:encoded:
Team Heretics has made yet another change to its LEC team this year with the signing of Erlend ‘Nukeduck’ Våtevik Holm as head coach, pending Riot Games’ approval.

The Norwegian reunites with Heretics’ assistant coach and Spain’s Esports Nations Cup LoL coach, Alfonso “Mithy” Aguirre Rodríguez.

This comes after the reported departure of assistant coach Emre ‘Arkhe’ Akpınarlı, with Heretics confirming the organisation will reveal more news about its coaching structure in the coming days.

The signing of Nukeduck also spells doubts over the future of Heretics’ current head coach, Jonas ‘Hidon’ Vraa, who is contracted with the team until after LoL Worlds 2027.

Nukeduck’s Impressive Track Record

Nukeduck is one of the longest-serving members of LoL esports, having been competing at a top level since Season 2 in 2012.

After a 10-year pro career, playing for Lemondogs, Team Vitality, Origen and more, Nukeduck turned to coaching in North America.

After a long stint with Team 100 Thieves, Nukeduck then found success with FlyQuest.

Working with Mithy, Nukeduck led FlyQuest to three LEC titles, qualified for two World Championships, and pushed Gen.G to five games in a very impressive series.

Nukeduck first worked with Mithy in 2013 and 2014 playing together on Lemondogs and Ninjas in Pyjamas.

They then both played for Origen in 2019 before reuniting as coaches in 2024.

Team Heretics’ LEC Woes

The 2026 LEC season has been a very challenging one for Team Heretics.

The team has failed to qualify for the Playoffs in both splits so far and was also eliminated from Esports World Cup qualification.

Heretics finished dead last in the recent Spring Split after winning just one series and three out of 20 matches played.

Despite coaching staff members claiming that Heretics wants to build a long-term project, the team has already undergone numerous roster changes this year.

Support Paul ‘Stend’ Lardin was dropped for Han ‘Way’ Gil, and jungler Théo ‘Sheo’ Borile benched himself, forcing Heretics to promote Kacper ‘Daglas’ Dagiel.

After his previous success, can Nukeduck turn things around at Team Heretics? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP
